records.ety: [L. universalis: cf. F. universel, OF. also universal. See Universe.]

defs: Of or pertaining to the universe; extending to, including, or affecting, the whole number, quantity, or space; unlimited; general; all-reaching; all-pervading; as, universal ruin; universal good; universal benevolence or benefice.
quotes: The universal cause Acts not by partial, but by general laws.
notes: ☞ Universal and its derivatives are used in common discourse for general and its derivatives. See General.

defs: Constituting or considered as a whole; total; entire; whole; as, the universal world.
quotes: At which the universal host up dent A shout that tore Hell's concave.

defs: Adapted or adaptable to all or to various uses, shapes, sizes, etc.; as, a universal milling machine.

Forming the whole of a genus; relatively unlimited in extension; affirmed or denied of the whole of a subject; as, a universal proposition; -- opposed to particular; e. g. (universal affirmative) All men are animals; (universal negative) No men are omniscient.
The whole; the general system of the universe; the universe.
quotes: Plato calleth God the cause and original, the nature and reason, of the universal.

defs: A general abstract conception, so called from being universally applicable to, or predicable of, each individual or species contained under it.
A universal proposition. See Universal, a., 4.
